title: IMD Issues Rain, Thunderstorm and Heatwave Alerts as Monsoon Advances Across India - illustrated Daily News
full_text:
Weather conditions across India are undergoing a significant transition as the southwest monsoon gains momentum, bringing rainfall to several regions while parts of northwestern India continue to grapple with intense heat and heatwave conditions. The India Meteorological Department (IMD) has issued multiple weather alerts for various states over the coming days, warning of heavy rainfall, thunderstorms, strong winds and lightning activity.
According to the IMD, a combination of active western disturbances, upper-air cyclonic circulations and other weather systems is expected to keep conditions unstable across large parts of the country. The advancing southwest monsoon is also likely to influence weather patterns in several regions during the next few days.
In northern India, rainfall activity is expected to continue over Uttarakhand, Himachal Pradesh and Jammu and Kashmir between June 11 and June 16. Several areas of eastern and western Uttar Pradesh, along with eastern Rajasthan, are also likely to witness rain accompanied by thunderstorms. The weather department has warned of strong winds reaching speeds of 40 to 50 kmph and the possibility of lightning in these regions.
Delhi-NCR is also expected to experience relatively pleasant weather conditions. The forecast indicates partly cloudy skies with chances of light to moderate rainfall and gusty winds, which could provide relief from the prevailing heat.
The IMD has issued an orange alert for heavy to very heavy rainfall in Arunachal Pradesh, Assam, Meghalaya, Sikkim and sub-Himalayan West Bengal. Some areas in these states could witness extremely heavy rainfall during the forecast period. Heavy rain is also likely in parts of Bihar, Telangana, Kerala, Karnataka, Tamil Nadu, Konkan and Goa.
The southwest monsoon continues to advance steadily across the country. Meteorologists expect it to cover the remaining parts of Maharashtra, Karnataka, Telangana, Andhra Pradesh and Tamil Nadu over the next five days. Conditions are also becoming favourable for the onset of the monsoon in West Bengal, Odisha, Jharkhand, Bihar and Chhattisgarh.
Apart from rainfall, the weather department has warned of thunderstorms, lightning strikes and strong winds in several states. Bihar and the Gangetic regions of West Bengal may experience wind speeds of 50 to 60 kmph. Similar weather conditions are likely in parts of Madhya Pradesh, Maharashtra, Jharkhand, Odisha, Chhattisgarh, Telangana, Tamil Nadu and Andhra Pradesh.
Weather experts have advised residents in vulnerable areas to remain cautious and follow official advisories, particularly in regions prone to lightning strikes, strong winds and heavy rainfall. While the advancing monsoon is expected to bring relief from scorching temperatures in many areas, severe weather events could pose challenges in several parts of the country over the coming days.
